ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'pizza' class (FOODON_00002572); no specific Pizza Margherita subclass exists. Closest parent match applied — the generic pizza class is the nearest defensible ancestor in the FoodOn hierarchy.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No Margherita-specific code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent match applied using the generic pizza category as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Pizza Margherita as a food concept.

main_ingredients: pizza dough (wheat flour, water, yeast, salt)

serving.description: 1 slice (approximately 1/8 of a standard 30 cm pizza)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one slice of Pizza Margherita weighing approximately 100 g, representing roughly one-eighth of a standard 30 cm pizza.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza Margherita — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pizza Margherita (cr_id G00010739). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pizza with mozzarella (FDC ID 1100483) as a supplementary reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database — Pizza, plain baked dough with tomato sauce and cheese
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI values for plain cheese pizza in the range of 60–80 (reference food: white bread = 100 scale). A GI of 80 is applied here based on the closest analogous entry for pizza with refined wheat dough, tomato sauce, and mozzarella. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (80 × 32.38) / 100 = 25.9 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.75 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium is moderate at 130 mg/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Pizza Margherita to 1–2 slices per meal, request reduced-sugar tomato sauce, and pair with a high-potassium side salad (e.g., spinach or avocado) to offset the insulin-mediated sodium retention driven by the high sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (32.38 g/100 g) and high sugar (15.75 g/100 g) with minimal dietary fibre (1.7 g/100 g) produces a high glycemic index (~80) and glycemic load (~25.9 per 100 g), driving rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict Pizza Margherita to a maximum of 1 slice, request a wholemeal or thin crust to reduce refined carbohydrate density, choose reduced-sugar tomato sauce, and pair with a high-fibre salad to blunt the postprandial glucose spike.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.8 g/100 g from mozzarella) and high sugar (15.75 g/100 g) contribute to atherogenic risk; cholesterol is low at 3 mg/100 g and sodium is moderate at 130 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should limit Pizza Margherita to 1–2 slices, favour thin-crust preparations to reduce total saturated fat and refined carbohydrate intake, and avoid adding processed meat toppings that would further elevate sodium and saturated fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fresh basil and olive oil provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols (eugenol, oleocanthal); however, tomato sauce contains nightshade compounds (solanine) that may aggravate symptoms in sensitive individuals, and high sugar (15.75 g/100 g) may promote inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should monitor joint symptoms after consuming Pizza Margherita; request reduced-sugar tomato sauce and limit portion to 1 slice to minimise pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Margherita is a classic Italian pizza originating from Naples, Italy, traditionally composed of a hand-stretched, fermented wheat dough base topped with tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella cheese, fresh basil leaves, and a drizzle of olive oil, then baked at high temperature. The crust is characteristically thin to medium in thickness, with a slightly charred, chewy texture and a light, airy crumb produced by the fermentation process. The flavour profile is simple and balanced: tangy tomato, creamy mild mozzarella, and herbaceous basil. Per 100 g, Pizza Margherita provides 204 kcal, 32.38 g carbohydrate (of which 15.75 g sugar), 7.53 g fat, 1.84 g protein, 1.7 g dietary fibre, and 130 mg sodium. The high sugar content relative to fibre (sugar:fibre ratio approximately 9:1) produces a high glycemic index (~80) and glycemic load (~25.9 per 100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and a moderate concern for those with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The olive oil and fresh basil contribute anti-inflammatory polyphenols (oleocanthal, eugenol), and the baked preparation avoids the advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) associated with deep-frying. In Singapore, Pizza Margherita is widely available at Italian restaurants and pizza chains and is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item. It is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010739) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous pizza entry. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous pizza entry. All primary values are from approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this Pizza Margherita preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010739) or confirmed in USDA FoodData Central for an exact match as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat (2.8 g/100 g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 1100483 (pizza with mozzarella, baked) as the closest approved-source analogue; this substitution is documented per source policy. GI of 80 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain cheese pizza on refined wheat dough; no exact Margherita-specific GI entry exists. Input cultural.primary_cuisine was incorrectly listed as 'Japanese'; corrected to 'Italian' based on established culinary origin. Pizza Margherita is an Italian dish; Japanese multilingual naming rules do not apply. No mandatory local-language names are required for Italian cuisine under the Veritas multilingual matrix. Iteration 3 fix: ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type corrected from 'broader' (not an allowed value) to 'closest_parent' (the correct allowed value for a parent-level match where no exact code exists for the specific dish).
